reasoning: The market probability around March 20 was 19%, driven by optimism over a US-brokered 30-day ceasefire proposal. However, by March 25-26, negotiations in Saudi Arabia concluded with Vladimir Putin explicitly rejecting the full 30-day ceasefire, instead agreeing only to a "limited" ceasefire concerning energy infrastructure and the Black Sea. The resolution criteria explicitly require an agreement that pertains to "all theaters of military conflict." While the White House reportedly still aims for a truce by April 20 (Easter), the rejection of the comprehensive ceasefire and the fundamental gaps between Russian and Ukrainian demands (e.g., Putin's preconditions amounting to Ukrainian surrender) make a comprehensive agreement highly unlikely within the remaining 20 days. Given the failure of the late March talks to secure a full ceasefire, the probability has significantly declined from its March 20 peak.
